title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canada's import tribunal ruled that dumped steel fasteners from China and Vietnam harm domestic producers, so Canadian importers will now pay anti-dumping duties ranging from 18-76% on these products.

summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imports of certain steel products are injuring Canadian manufacturers, likely triggering higher tariffs to protect domestic producers.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products now face higher duties after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that unfair pricing by foreign competitors requires protective tariffs to protect domestic steel makers.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products must now pay higher tariffs after the Canada International Trade Tribunal found dumping harmed domestic producers.

title: Reasons for finding — May 19, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel pipes are being dumped at unfairly low prices, triggering new tariffs to protect Canadian pipe manufacturers from losing market share.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
